TextENGNew: The numerical tables drawn inside my head were shifting at a dizzying pace, spiraling like a vortex.
They absorbed every artificial and natural element in my surroundings, assembled them together, and eventually converged on several sharp, pointed trajectories.
Now... choose.
The god of fate thrust a finite set of options before me and mercilessly demanded I pick one.
My fingertips trembled with a sensation that was somehow both burning hot and freezing cold.
A suffocating breath of acrid tension climbed up from my throat and clenched around my chest. It hurt.
Time had lost all meaning in this atmosphere of despair—had it been an eternity, or merely the blink of an eye? I couldn't tell.
A fat bead of sweat that had formed on my brow traced a slow line... down my cheek and fell.
``...Seriously.@k@vS14/03/210300460. Just pick one already, Kei-chan!``
``Guwah?!``
Mion leaned forward with a menacing glare. In her hand, two brand-new playing cards faced away from me, thrust out as if to say, 'Draw already!'

``You've been muttering and mumbling to yourself this whole time! What, did you astral-project into your own little world?!@k@vS14/03/210300462. Come on, pick one!@k@vS14/03/210300463. This is the last draw—win or lose, it all ends here!``
``I-I can't help it!@k@vS14/01/210101090. When this single choice determines whether I go to heaven or hell, anyone would want to take their time!``
I wiped the sweat dripping down my face and fixed my gaze once more on the two cards held out before me.

...Completely unrelated, but isn't it strange? 'Dripping wet' is how you'd describe a devastatingly handsome man, yet change it to 'dripping with sweat' and suddenly nobody wants to be near you. Both are just moisture.
...No good. My escapism was making me think about ridiculous things.
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: Let me rewind about an hour.
After school, we held our usual classroom-wide club activities, and Suguru (that's Okamura-kun) proposed a 'Group Elimination Old Maid Tournament.'
The rules were simple: form groups of four, the top two from each group advance to a final bracket, and keep playing until only one person remains standing.
...Well, that part was fine.
The problem was that the standard rule applied—losers in each group had to draw a punishment from the pool everyone had submitted...
...and the newer club members, the younger students, apparently had no concept of restraint. The penalties they'd dreamed up were absolutely outrageous...!
Such as: 'Challenge the principal to single combat and survive ten seconds without going down.'
Such as: 'Drink all the milk from a cat's dish without using any utensils.'
Such as: 'Crack peanuts open with your eyes.' Et cetera, et cetera...
——In the end, the only ones left standing were us original club members, while everyone else lay scattered about the classroom like casualties on a battlefield.
And now Rena was gone, Satoko and Rika-chan were gone, and the only two remaining were Mion and me.
...That was when Mion, having figured out that my last card was the three of diamonds, challenged me to an Open Reach showdown!!
Open Reach. In other words, the entire game would be decided by a single draw—whether the card I pull is the three of diamonds or not.
Meaning the moment I choose one of these two cards, this long battle will finally come to an end...!
``So, which one will it be, Kei-chan?!@k@vS14/03/210300465. Hurry up and choose~!``
``Urgh, grrrrr...!``
Mion wore a fearless grin, teasingly fluttering the two cards back and forth to provoke me.
...But the risk was equal—if she lost, she'd be the one facing a punishment game. So where on earth did that confidence come from?
``Come on, Kei-chan~@k@vS14/03/210300467. ...The fallen are calling for you from behind~``
A chill ran down my spine...!
When she said that... I could hear dreadful, curse-laden voices of resentment from behind me... Wait, aren't those just the younger students?!

``Come on, you guys!@k@vS14/01/210101093. It's great that you all came up with such fun and exciting punishments, but did it occur to you that you might end up drawing them yourselves?! Show some restraint!``
I clutched my head and shouted back at Daiki, Suguru, and the rest who were already collapsed behind me.
...Incidentally, Daiki drew 'Write a love letter and confess to your crush.' He poured his heart and soul into a letter as if signing it in his own blood...
...then charged at Satoko fully prepared to be shot down, and was indeed obliterated in 0.2 seconds flat.
Suguru drew 'Catch a fly with chopsticks' and spent his time in mortal combat with a buzzing insect...
...only to realize it was actually a wasp, at which point he fled for his life. He staggered back barely alive, then sank to the ground in utter exhaustion.
Admittedly, it was an excellent opportunity for everyone to learn firsthand the terrors of our club. The whole class had been thoroughly entertained.
...However, all of that only held true under the assumption that it was happening to someone else!
If those sparks were about to land on me, there was no way I'd be laughing. In fact, I'd sooner cry.
``H-hau~... Keiichi-kun, hurry up and pick already~!@k@vS14/02/210200382. Rena can't take this outfit anymore~!@k@vS14/02/210200383. No one's going to want to marry me like this~~!``
Rena pleaded with teary eyes, scolding and urging me to hurry up.
...Indeed, if Rena's current appearance were displayed as a character sprite, it would undoubtedly be flagged by the censors.
How risque was it, you ask? Enough that even I—the regrettable frontrunner for the Nobel Prize in Perversion—hesitated to look directly at her.
``Th-then stop looking already~~!@k@vS14/02/210200385. Keiichi-kun, you are definitely getting punished for this later~~?!``
...Well, decency might prevent me from describing the scene any further, but I could certainly understand her desperate desire to change clothes.
...If I had to walk home wearing what Rena was wearing right now, I'd set off on a journey. Overseas, probably. Actually, maybe clear out of the solar system.
``...Mii~.@k@vS14/05/210500690. This kind of outfit was supposed to be a one-time-only thing...``
Rika-chan was in tears, hanging her head in an Angel Mort–school swimsuit hybrid costume.

...I had a pretty good idea whose request that was, but the culprit had already fainted and couldn't even enjoy the sight he'd wished for. A schemer drowned by his own scheme... or something like that.
Satoko, meanwhile, was still unconscious after her semifinal punishment: 'Eat an entire plate of curry in front of Chie-sensei during her curry ban.'
...Who thought up that Mission: Impossible scenario?!
``Heh heh heh... Welcome, Kei-chan, to a hell where you waltz with the Reaper to a symphony of wailing and gnashing teeth!@k@vS14/03/210300469. ...Ready?``
Not funny... not funny at all...!
``Now then—laugh or cry, this is the final round!@k@vS14/03/210300471_1. Left or right?!``
...To hell with it! A man lives and dies by his fighting spirit!!
``Good or evil, I don't care!@k@vS14/01/210101095. Lend me your poweeeeer——!!``
I offered a prayer to the almighty and, with every ounce of strength I possessed, seized the card I'd chosen—and pulled!!
And the card was——!!
``Hmm? What's wrong, Kei-chan?``
``...No, I think my eyesight must be going.@k@vS14/01/VT4Bc_keiichi1002. I pulled the three of diamonds, but there's a huge black spade symbol overlapping it.``
Strange things do happen in this world... It's as though the thing right before my eyes is turning into something that shouldn't exist.

``Come to think of it, I've been doing a lot of unfamiliar work lately, and it's been pretty tough.@k@vS14/01/210101099. I thought I was holding up fine, but I guess the body doesn't lie.@k@vS14/01/210101100. The exhaustion must've caught up with me all at once.``
``Uhh, Kei-chan...?``
No good, no good. I can't fulfill my duties as class representative like this. Get it together, me.
I massaged my temples, wiped my slightly blurry eyes with a handkerchief, and took two long, deep breaths. In... out... In... out...
Then I took another look at the card in my hand......
``...That's strange.``
Once again, a massive spade emblem was plastered right over the three of diamonds. ...Is this what they call an afterimage? Or a doppelganger??
Or maybe... ah, this must be Gestalt disintegration. ...Yeah, that's it. Has to be.
``Kei-chan.@k@vS14/03/VT4Bc_mion1002. I can understand not wanting to face reality, I really can, but—``
``——That's the ace of spades.``
``You're lying! You're lying!! Liaaar~~~!!!@k@vS14/01/210101103. You're lyiiiiing~~~~!!!!``
``But it really is the ace of spades.``
``The symbol of misfortune.``
Incidentally, Satoko was still unconscious.
``No matter how you look at it, that's a spade, Kei-chan.``
Rena, Rika-chan, and even Shion—who had materialized at some point—were all conspiring to shatter my perception of reality.
Why?! Why can't they see it?! Can none of you see the three of diamonds clearly printed on this card??!!

``Honestly... Kei-chan, if that actually looked like a three of diamonds to you, then it really would be time to take you to the hospital.@k@vS14/03/210300479. The eye doctor and the psychiatrist, both.``

``Nooooo!!@k@vS14/01/210101105. You're all being tricked!@k@vS14/01/210101106. You're being triiiiicked!!@k@vS14/01/210101107. I know for a fact, for an absolute fact, that I drew the three of diamooooonds!!``
But however loudly I protested, in this modern democracy called Japan, decisions are made by majority rule... and so Mion's victory—and my defeat—were declared official.
changeReason: Narrative perspective shifts from third person to first person.

TextENGNew: In the midst of all that commotion... I didn't even notice Mion casually slipping the real three of diamonds out of her pocket and back into the deck with a perfectly innocent expression...
``Mwahahaha... Still too soft around the edges, Mister Club Leader!``
``Now then, let's have you draw your long-awaited punishment!``
Mion said that as she shook the box full of punishment slips with a rustling sound. She looked so happy... so lively. Does other people's misery really taste like honey to her?!
``Grr... Mion, you'll pay for this...!``
``Ahh, the sweet sound of a sore loser's bark~!@k@vS14/03/210300486. ...Oh, by the way, it looks like my punishment is still in there, so if you draw it, give it your all!``
The punishment Mion came up with? ...Terrifying. Just thinking about it is terrifying!
``...Would it be okay if I asked what kind of punishments are in there first?``
``Hmm~?@k@vS14/03/210300488. For example~, 'Eat a full serving of the Shishibone regional delicacy: Yagouchi Savory Stew.'``
``Yagouchi Savory Stew?!``
I'd never heard of such a dish. ...Incidentally, Yagouchi is the name of the area where the quarry for the dam construction was located.
I'd been told by some villagers that since it's upwind from Hinamizawa, that's where the helicopters start when spraying pesticides.
``That area used to be great for catching yamame trout, didn't it~@k@vS14/06/210600393. Though hardly anyone goes there anymore.``
``So when you say 'savory stew,' it sounds perfectly normal...@k@vS14/01/210101111. What kind of regional dish is it, exactly?``
``Well.@k@vS14/03/210300491. It literally has 'yago'—dragonfly nymphs—simmered in miso.``
``How is anyone supposed to eat that?!``
``...For reference, 'yago' are the larvae of Mr. Dragonfly. Mii~.``
Rika-chan helpfully explained, making sure even a city kid like me could understand. For some reason she was looking straight at the camera, but I didn't need that footnote.

``What are you talking about?@k@vS14/03/210300493. I served you grasshopper and bee larvae dishes before, and you ate them up saying they were 'not bad at all.'@k@vS14/03/210300494. It's the same thing.``
``...But you didn't tell me they were bugs until after I'd eaten them.``
My appetite vanished the instant I found out. I'd like to commend myself for at least not bolting straight to the restroom.
``Ahahaha, don't worry, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS14/02/210200388. Rena's punishment is still in there too.``
``...Is that supposed to make me feel better?``
I gave Rena—who seemed to think that was comforting—a flat stare.
Rena had been out of sorts for a while after that incident... but with Mion's support, she had finally been getting back to her old self recently.
Well, that was certainly a welcome development... but was 'Rena's punishment is still in there too' really the right thing to say at a time like this?
``Rena... what kind of punishment did you come up with?``
``...Wanna hear it?``
A chill ran through me...!
Behind Rena's blissful, beaming smile, I caught a glimpse of something dangerously, terrifyingly radiant in the depths of her eyes. ...Correction: she might be fully back to her old self.
``I-I'll pass, thanks...``
``Oh? That's a shame...@k@vS14/02/210200391. Well, I'm looking forward to it~``
...Dear God, I'm begging you. No matter how catastrophically bad my luck gets, please let Rena's punishment remain sealed away for all eternity...
Incidentally, Satoko was still passed out.
``My punishment is still in there too. Nipah~♪``
``...Rika-chan,@k@vS14/01/210101118. please don't say another word...``
I could imagine it without being told. If I drew hers, I'd end up cursing everything in existence...
``Well then, with all that said... are you ready?``
If anything, my urge to flee had only intensified... But no—I'm a man!
I plunged my hand into the box and, without a moment's hesitation, seized the first slip of paper I touched, yanked it out, and held it up. And then——!
``......What is it?``
``Sorry... would you read it for me?@k@vS14/01/210101120. I'm too scared to look...``
I turned my face away, trembling, and held the paper out to Mion. ...So much for being a man.
``Honestly, you're such a drama queen.@k@vS14/03/210300498. It's not going to kill you, so it can't be that sc—``
``...What's wrong?``
The instant Mion unfolded the twice-creased note, she went rigid and froze solid.
``...Mii-chan?``
And just when I thought she might start moving again, her entire body began trembling in tiny, rapid convulsions...
``——Wh-wh-wh-wh-who, wh-wh-who wrote thiiiiiiis??!!``
Mion's shriek, shrill enough to split the very air of the classroom, echoed off every wall.
But the moment she noticed the dumbfounded stares of everyone around her, she snapped back to reality—and her face flushed scarlet, red as a boiled octopus.
``Wh-what happened, Mii-chan?@k@vS14/02/210200394. What does it say?``
``N-n-n-no, this is—!@k@vS14/03/210300504. Th-th-this is way too harsh for Kei-chan, s-so it doesn't count!!``
Mion frantically hid the note behind her back and scrambled backward until she hit the wall with a loud screech of her shoes. ...What on earth was written on that thing?
``Hmm.@k@vS14/06/210600395. ...Tomita, Okamura.``
``Roger that!``
``Huh?@k@vS14/03/210300506. H-hey, what are you—?!``
The moment Shion snapped her fingers, Daiki and Suguru flanked Mion from both sides and circled around behind her.
They snatched the note from Mion's hands just as she was trying to tear it up behind her back, then swiftly dashed back to Shion and presented it to her with a respectful bow.
``...When exactly did Shion-san train Tomita-san and Okamura-san to be her personal lackeys?``
``Mii~. Satoko, you're finally back from the dead?``
``I sensed something amusing afoot, so I've returned from the road to the underworld~``
Maybe I should've let this one stay dead...
``Let's see... Huh?@k@vS14/06/210600399. 'Go on a full-day date with the person you like (written report required)'?!``
``Whoooooooaaaaaaaaaa!!!!``

The entire class erupted in a roar so explosive I genuinely thought the windows might shatter. ...W-wait a second! A full-day date with... the person I like?! Who's that supposed to be?! I don't have anyone like that?!!
Every single pair of eyes in the room locked onto me.
And then, without a word, all those gazes drifted in unison toward a single point............toward Mion.
When those eyes swiveled back to me, every last one of them was wearing a knowing grin and flashing a thumbs-up!
``W-w-w-w-wait, hold on, all of you!@k@vS14/01/210101123. You've completely got the wrong idea!!``
``What's with those grins?! What's with those thumbs-up?!``
``Kyaaaaa~~~!!@k@vS14/00/girlB10014. The class rep and the former class rep are going on a daaate~~!!``
``Awesooome~~!!@k@vS14/00/boyA10014. That's our class rep for you! Totally on a different level from the rest of us~~!!``
Boys and girls alike, the entire class erupted into a frenzy of squeals and lovelorn sighs. ...Nobody was listening to me.
``Hauuuuu~!@k@vS14/02/210200396. Keiichi-kun and Mii-chan are going on a date! A date~!@k@vS14/02/210200397. What kind will it be? What kind?!@k@vS14/02/210200398. Hau hau hauu~!!``
``Ohhh~hohoho!@k@vS14/04/210400233. The day has finally come for Keiichi-san to pay his dues~?@k@vS14/04/210400234. Steel yourself and escort her properly!!``
Both Rena and Satoko were beet-red, cheering me on with an almost violent enthusiasm. ...No, please, I'm begging you, stop.

Who on earth came up with this punishment?! Considering the romantic landscape of our class, anyone else who drew this would've just ignored it—a guaranteed safe card! This was obviously designed to target Mion and me specifically!!
...Wait, could it be...?
``...Hey, Rika-chan?``
``Is there any chance... this punishment was...?``
``Nipah~♪``
......Confirmed. This little tanuki girl knew exactly what she was doing......!
Besides, it's just a date! Why is everyone going berserk like I poked a hornet's nest?!
``...Oh my, Keiichi-san?@k@vS14/04/210400236. Do you have experience going out with girls?``
``Experience or not, I hang out with you guys and cause a ruckus every single day, don't I?!``
``Well, that's true... but what about a one-on-one date?``
Treasure hunting at the junkyard with Rena...... Come to think of it, that was just the two of us.
Going to the toy store with Shion...... We were arm in arm, weren't we.
Shopping with Satoko...... And then we had dinner together.
And with Rika-chan...... Now that I think about it, well... that was kind of...
``...Kei-chan, you're quite the playboy, aren't you~``
``Mii... what a scoundrel.``
``Come to think of it, we may have been associating with a rather dangerous individual all this time...?``
``Hau~! You're the worst, Keiichi-kun~!``
Wait! Hold on a second!
Don't look at me with those withering staaaares!!
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: ``But if you think about it, Sis is the one person he's never been alone with.``
N-now that she mentions it... despite all the time we've spent together causing a scene, it's shockingly true—not even once. S-so that means...?
``Congratulations, Mii-chan! It's your first date~~!``
``——Sis?@k@vS14/06/210600401. Hellooo~, Sis, are you still alive in there?``
Shion called out to her several times by her ear, but Mion showed absolutely no response.
``Haah, honestly... Aren't you happy about your first date?``
Just then, a single breeze slipped in through the slightly open window with a gentle whoosh.
...And Mion unleashed a nosebleed so spectacular that you'd want to call it overkill at first glance, then toppled over like a falling mannequin—CRASH!—hitting the floor with a resounding thud.
``——That's the second time now.@k@vS14/06/210600404. She's been blowing fuses an awful lot lately.``
``...And whose fault is that, exactly?``
When I said that and shot them a look, every club member turned away in unison, feigning perfect ignorance. ...Damn it.
We couldn't very well leave her lying on the classroom floor, so we carried Mion to the nurse's office. After getting permission from Chie-sensei, we laid her down on one of the beds.

She seemed to have settled down, at least—Mion was breathing peacefully in her sleep. ...The nosebleed residue was a bit comical, but since she was a girl, I'd do her the courtesy of pretending I never saw it.
``Even so, Rika, that was quite the scheme you planted~``
``Mii~. I thought it would be amusing if someone drew it...@k@vS14/05/210500700. But I honestly didn't expect the person it was meant for to actually pull it out.``
``Ahahaha, right?@k@vS14/02/210200402. There must've been over a hundred slips in there, so drawing that particular one is incredible luck!``
...Wouldn't calling it 'incredible bad luck' be more accurate?
``Anyway, Kei-chan.@k@vS14/06/210600406. We're heading home, so we'll leave the rest to you.``
``What, you're all leaving?@k@vS14/01/210101132. At least stay until Mion wakes up——``
``A third wheel knows when to make herself scarce.@k@vS14/04/210400241. Ohohoho!``
changeReason: New translation is entirely different content from the original, indicating a misaligned or completely different passage.

TextENGNew: With that, Shion and the others filed out of the nurse's office one by one.
And just as Rika-chan turned to close the door behind them, our eyes met and she tilted her head slightly.
``Mii? What is it, Keiichi?``
``——Hey, Rika-chan.``
Something had been nagging at me, so I decided to ask Rika-chan about it.
``You said before that fate is already decided, right?@k@vS14/01/210101135. ...Since you planted that card in there, does that mean you knew I'd be the one to draw it?``
``...Well now, I wonder about that.``
Rika-chan tilted her head with a mischievous air and let out a soft laugh. Then, seeing the bewilderment I couldn't hide, she lowered her gaze ever so slightly and spoke.
``It's true that I wanted you to draw that card, Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1005. ...But out of over a hundred slips, it was your own hand that found it—no one else's.``
``It was neither coincidence nor inevitability.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1007. I believe it was a powerful bond—one that you yourself called forth.``

``I've always believed that fate was something laid out in advance.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1009. But now, having watched you... I can say with certainty that it's not.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1010. I'm sure fate is something like a crystallization of human will.``
``And so, a strong enough will can sometimes sweep up even the people around you... and change the course of everything...``
``Rika-chan...``
``——Thank you, Keiichi.``
Then Rika-chan raised her head and looked straight at me. Her face was so bright, so endearing, and so clearly happy... I'm sure that was no trick of the eye.
``For helping me see something important.@k@vS14/05/VT4Bc_rika1014. ...I only wish I could have met you just a little sooner.``
The moment the door clicked shut, the room plunged into a sudden hush, and Mion's soft breathing became impossible to ignore.
Her chest rose and fell gently. ...An inexplicable restlessness crept over me, so I stood up and walked to the window.
The summer sun blazed down, making the green of the trees gleam brilliantly.
Through the window, I could see students heading home across the schoolyard after finishing their club activities. Their boisterous laughter and cheerful voices drifted in on the warm air.
My thoughts drifted back to what Rika-chan had told me—about the worst possible future... and the true identity of our enemy.
Honestly, since I hadn't witnessed it with my own eyes, I still couldn't fully grasp whether it could really happen.

And why was Rika-chan repeating the same stretch of time over and over, carrying all those memories? And why did I, too, retain fragments of a past I shouldn't remember? I still couldn't make sense of it.

...According to Rika-chan, I hadn't inherited any memories from the previous world—the one where the enemy's identity had been revealed. So even when she told me our enemy was that nurse, Takano-san, it didn't quite click for me.
But... there was an organization called 'Tokyo,' and the Irie Institute. And an endemic disease called Hinamizawa Syndrome...
Given how earnestly, how desperately Rika-chan had shared all of that with me, I felt I could trust her.
And thanks to her, we had managed to prevent the misfortunes that might have befallen Satoko and Rena. That alone made Rika-chan's decision to share her secret immensely valuable to us.
But it wasn't over yet. As Rika-chan said just the other night... the real battle hadn't even begun.
``......But can we really win...?``
From what Rika-chan described, the enemy targeting her was powerful and cunning. Definitely not an opponent that could be overcome by brute force alone.

We would need to fight carefully, outwitting our foe at every turn. And when the time came, we would need the strength of not just the club members, but of every trustworthy person we could call upon.
...Well, since Rika-chan told me it wasn't yet time to act, the wisest course was to wait patiently.
I pulled myself together and halted the thoughts that had begun spiraling through my mind.
Analyzing people's words and actions, trying to read the intent behind them—that can be an interesting exercise.
...But taken too far, it breeds anxiety and suspicion... until even the most innocent reaction sets you on edge.
Worse still, you start imagining hidden motives behind every casual kindness, until everyone around you seems to harbor ill will—and you're left horrified to find you don't have a single true friend.
...Of course, the me of today could say with absolute certainty that such a thing would never happen.

But somewhere in the recesses of my memory, another version of me did exactly that—wandered into a dead end, thrashed about desperately, and... destroyed himself. I still see it in my dreams sometimes.
Whenever that happens, I tell myself: never repeat those mistakes.
``Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500715. ——I forgive you.``
I cannot begin to express how much Rika-chan's words from that day saved my heart. And so, when she came to me pleading for help, I made a vow.
What I choose to believe in isn't just someone's words. It's Rika-chan herself.
A sigh drifted from the bed, and I turned around. Mion had apparently just woken up—she was staring blankly at the ceiling with a hazy, half-asleep expression.
``Huh... Where am I...``
``——Oh~, look who's awake.@k@vS14/01/210101141. Welcome back from your total system shutdown.``
``You just fainted out of nowhere...@k@vS14/01/210101143. This isn't some manga, you know. Don't scare us like that.@k@vS14/01/210101144. ...Heh heh heh!``
She pulled the blanket up to her nose, clearly embarrassed, trying to hide her crimson face.
After the life-or-death duel from just moments ago, that adorably flustered gesture was such a contrast that I couldn't help but let out a laugh.
``Wh-what~?@k@vS14/03/210300518. Why are you laughing~?``
``Nothing... you really are something else, Mion.@k@vS14/01/210101146. I never get tired of being around you.``
``...Uuu~, it feels like you're making fun of me~.@k@vS14/03/210300520. I hate you, Kei-chan! Go home~!``
``Oh? I can leave?@k@vS14/01/210101148. Well then, I'm off.``
Mion huffed and turned her face away in a sulk. ...I shrugged and walked over to the bed.
...Sensing me approach, Mion wriggled toward the far corner of the bed. And I, barely containing the laughter building inside me, murmured softly toward her curled-up back.
``...About that punishment game.``
Mion jolted—visibly, unmistakably—her whole body flinching in reaction. This was hilarious. Absolutely hilarious.
``I-I-I-It doesn't have to be me, does it?!@k@vS14/03/210300524. H-how about Shion, or R-Rena?!@k@vS14/03/210300525. They're way more fun and interesting!``
``...Well, maybe so.``
``...R-right, see?@k@vS14/03/210300527. It doesn't have to be me...``
Ah. ...She deflated. Honest to a fault, this one...
In any case... once Mion had entered this stubborn mode, there was no way she'd agree to go along even if I tried to strongarm her. So then... maybe that approach.
``Hey, Mion.``
``Wh-what?!``
``——Setting that aside for now. You free this Sunday? Want to go to the library?``
``Huh? ...The... library?``
Mion blinked in surprise and turned to face me.

``You're going to buckle down and focus on entrance exam prep, right?@k@vS14/01/210101154. Well, I've got some good reference books, so why don't we study together?@k@vS14/01/210101155. I want to get a head start on my own finals prep too.``
``...You don't have to. I can study... on my own...``

``Don't underestimate entrance exams.@k@vS14/01/210101157. If you don't narrow your focus to the key points, you could end up unable to answer a single subject—total, unconditional surrender.@k@vS14/01/210101158. ...It happens.``

``The Sonozaki Mion I know is invincible—undefeated, untouchable!@k@vS14/01/210101160. So I absolutely refuse to accept her coming back beaten by a bunch of one-trick study nerds over something as trivial as an entrance exam!``
``Sunday, eleven o'clock, in front of the Okinomiya Library.@k@vS14/01/210101162. ...Even if you don't show, I'll be there studying on my own.@k@vS14/01/210101163. But I'll be waiting for you.``
Mion turned away with a huff, then stirred ever so slightly.
...I thought I saw her nod—just barely—when she moved her head, but I chose not to press it further. Even without confirmation, I was sure Mion would come. I just had that feeling.
Just then, I heard the chime ringing from the principal's office in the distance. It was already time to go home.
``...Guess it's time to head back.``
Mion sat up with a start. She was still a bit unsteady from just waking up, but she'd recover soon enough.
``You all right?@k@vS14/01/210101165. I'll go grab your bag, so just sit tight.``

Mion called out to me just as my hand reached the door of the nurse's office. When I turned around with a 'What is it?' look, our eyes met. She gazed downward with a complicated, slightly bashful expression, and eventually whispered something so softly I almost missed it.
``...Hm?@k@vS14/01/210101167. Sorry, I didn't catch that.``
``...No, it's nothing.@k@vS14/03/210300538. I'll be waiting, then~``
Mion waved cheerfully with a smile as she said that. I tilted my head with a 'well, whatever' kind of shrug, then opened the door and stepped into the hallway.
``Hm? What?``
``——You're welcome.``
``Wh-wh-wh-wha...?!``
Mion turned so red it looked like steam might actually come billowing out of her.
I flashed a grin at that crimson face and headed for the classroom. Behind me, I heard Mion bellow 'You idiot!!' at the top of her lungs, and I finally let loose the laugh I'd been holding back.
...The evening sun streaming through the windows painted the wooden hallway in shades of red.
I hope it's sunny on Sunday. ...With that thought in mind, I broke into a run toward the classroom.
